description.value: AWS's agentic, spec-driven IDE (and companion CLI) that turns prompts into requirements, design, and task markdown files before generating code.

repository.comment: The public GitHub repo is an issue tracker and docs hub; the IDE and CLI source are not open-sourced there.

creator.comment: Introduced by AWS in July 2025 (product leads Nikhil Swaminathan and Deepak Singh, VP DevEx & Agents); copyright ©2026 Amazon.com, Inc.

latest-release.comment: IDE v0.11.132 patch released April 17, 2026 (v0.11 major on March 11, 2026). Checked 2026-04-21.

license.comment: Kiro IDE/CLI is proprietary AWS software. The IDE is based on Code OSS (VS Code's open-source core) but Kiro itself has no public LICENSE and source is not distributed.

pricing-model.comment: Free tier with 50 credits/month, then Pro $20, Pro+ $40, Power $200 per month. All paid plans are hosted SaaS with pay-per-use overage at $0.04/credit.

open-source.comment: Based on Code OSS and supports Open VSX plugins, but Kiro's own IDE/CLI code is not OSI-licensed or published.

covered-phases.comment: Spec workflow produces requirements.md (spec), design.md (architecture + data flow/schemas), tasks.md (plans + discrete tasks), then agent implementation.

methodology-style.comment: Positioned explicitly as "spec-driven development"; the three spec files are the central artifact from which code is generated.

opinionated.comment: The default flow prescribes the requirements → design → tasks sequence with fixed filenames. Ad-hoc chat is available but the spec-driven path is the framework's core.

ceremony-level.comment: Three prescribed markdown artifacts per feature plus steering files — more ceremony than pure chat, lighter than full BDD/ADR stacks.

mechanization-level.comment: Standalone IDE and CLI execute the full workflow; event-driven hooks automate file-save/PR/tool-use actions end-to-end.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Users review and edit requirements.md, design.md, and tasks.md before each next step; tasks are executed one at a time with status tracking.

approval-gates.comment: Each of the three spec artifacts is produced as a reviewable checkpoint before the next phase; implementation tasks are opt-in per task.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Default spec workflow requires user review at each phase. Hooks and headless CLI enable unattended runs for narrow tasks but not idea-to-merge by default.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration has phase-level human approvals between the three spec artifacts and per-task execution.

rollback-support.comment: No built-in revert mechanism for generated artifacts or code changes documented; relies on underlying git for rollback.

supported-agents.comment: Kiro ships its own first-party agent runtime; it does not layer on top of Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc. Uses Anthropic Claude models (Sonnet 4/4.5, Haiku 4.5, Opus 4.5/4.6/4.7) via its Auto agent.

editor-integrations.comment: Standalone IDE is a Code OSS fork (inherits the VS Code extension UX and Open VSX marketplace, but is not a VS Code extension). CLI added in v0.6 (November 2025) and expanded to headless CI/CD in CLI 2.0.

model-agnostic.comment: Model picker is restricted to Anthropic Claude variants (Sonnet 4, Sonnet 4.5, Haiku 4.5, Opus 4.5/4.6/4.7) plus an Auto mode that mixes them; no other vendors are exposed.

mcp-support.comment: Built-in MCP server support in both IDE and CLI; v0.11 added an MCP Registry Governance feature for org-wide allowlists.

artifact-formats.comment: Specs and steering are markdown (steering allows optional YAML frontmatter); custom agent definitions are JSON files in .kiro/agents/.

artifacts-covered.comment: requirements.md → specs, design.md → architecture, tasks.md → plans + tasks. No first-class ADR, changelog, or release-notes artifact.

project-layout.value: .kiro/ folder at workspace root, containing specs/ (requirements.md, design.md, tasks.md per feature), steering/ (product.md, tech.md, structure.md, plus user additions), agents/ (JSON agent configs), and hooks. Global equivalents live in ~/.kiro/.

artifacts-in-git.comment: All spec, steering, and agent files live in the workspace .kiro/ directory and are intended to be committed alongside source.

language-agnostic.comment: Workflow prescribes artifacts, not a language; spec examples include TypeScript interfaces and API schemas but the tool targets any stack.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Steering files are instructional; agent hooks fire on file events and pre/post tool use; each spec phase is a manual-approval checkpoint.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Workflow encourages requirements → design → tasks but users can edit any spec file directly at any time; there is no hard refusal to skip ahead.

artifact-validation.comment: Spec files are free-form markdown with suggested structure; no schema validation or structural rule enforcement.

memory-implementation.comment: Steering files (.kiro/steering/*.md and ~/.kiro/steering/*.md) plus AGENTS.md compatibility provide the persistent context layer.

persistent-memory.comment: Steering files persist across sessions and are auto-included; spec folders also persist per feature without manual handoff.

project-context-files.comment: Uses .kiro/steering/product.md, tech.md, structure.md by default and also honors AGENTS.md.

cost-tracking.comment: Credits per prompt are displayed in real time; monthly credit quotas effectively cap spend, with pay-per-use overage at $0.04/credit.

run-history.comment: Could not confirm whether Kiro stores structured run history/traces beyond the live chat; public docs do not describe a run-history/trace store. Checked 2026-04-21.

telemetry-opt.comment: Telemetry is disclosed in the FAQ and opt-out is available for Free and individual subscribers; enterprise (IAM Identity Center) accounts have telemetry disabled by default.

custom-templates.comment: Users can add custom steering files to direct the agent; custom agents with their own prompts are also supported.

hooks.comment: Agent Hooks fire on file save/create/delete, user prompt submission, agent-turn completion, pre/post tool use, and pre/post spec-task execution; hooks can run shell commands or agent prompts.

sub-agents.comment: Custom agents defined as JSON in .kiro/agents/ (workspace) or ~/.kiro/agents/ (global) with name, prompt, tools, mcpServers, allowedTools, etc.

marketplace.comment: No public marketplace for Kiro steering/agent/hook templates found as of 2026-04-21. Open VSX marketplace is inherited for generic VS Code extensions; MCP Registry is org-internal.

install-mechanism.comment: IDE is a downloadable installer for macOS/Windows/Linux; CLI installs via `curl -fsSL https://cli.kiro.dev/install | bash`.

setup-time.comment: Download installer or run the CLI install script, then log in with GitHub/Google/AWS Builder ID/IAM Identity Center.

self-hosted.comment: Inference and account/billing backend are AWS-hosted; the IDE/CLI are local but cannot be used without the managed service.

requires-account.comment: Login with GitHub, Google, AWS Builder ID, or AWS IAM Identity Center is required to use Kiro.

git-worktree-support.comment: No documented first-party git worktree integration. Kiro operates on the open workspace folder; whether multiple worktrees work in parallel is unverified. Checked 2026-04-21.

documentation-quality.comment: Well-structured docs site covering IDE and CLI with guides, reference, changelogs, and "Learn by playing" tutorials; some feature internals (hook storage format, run history) are thin.

github-stars.comment: The kirodotdev/Kiro repo (issues+docs only, ~3.5k stars on 2026-04-21) is not the product's source code. Per RESEARCH.md, stars are recorded as null for closed-source platforms.

active-maintenance.comment: IDE patch v0.11.132 shipped 2026-04-17, four days before this research; well within the 90-day threshold.

example-projects.comment: "Learn by playing" tutorial series and launch-blog walkthroughs demonstrate the full spec workflow on sample projects.

community-channel.comment: Official Discord at discord.gg/kirodotdev linked from the GitHub README.
